question_text: Does the Masonry routing system handle the case where all four routing layers fail? Is there a safe fallback that prevents infinite loops or silent drops?
rationale: Layer 4 is explicitly 'fallback: return target_agent=user' — asks for clarification. No infinite loop possible by design.

question_text: Can the BrickLayer research loop run indefinitely without human intervention? What stops it from running forever and consuming unbounded resources?
rationale: The loop runs until PENDING questions are exhausted. New questions can be generated by hypothesis-generator, creating a potential infinite loop. No hard session limit is enforced.

question_text: If the scored_all.jsonl training corpus has only records scoring in the 55-65 range with no examples above 75 or below 50, can the improve_agent optimizer make meaningful improvements?
rationale: The optimizer requires contrast between high (>=75) and low (<50) quality examples to identify what distinguishes good from bad outputs. Without contrast, the optimization prompt has no signal to learn from.

question_text: Does the Masonry MCP server gracefully handle the case where the Ollama semantic routing service at 192.168.50.62:11434 is unavailable?
rationale: The four-layer routing degrades gracefully — if semantic layer fails, it falls through to LLM routing (layer 3), then fallback (layer 4). Service unavailability does not crash the system.

question_text: Is the BrickLayer campaign finding file format stable enough that findings written by Wave 1 agents can be read correctly by Wave 2 synthesizer agents without schema migration?
rationale: Findings are markdown files with consistent header sections (verdict, severity, summary, evidence). No binary format or version field — inherently backwards compatible.

question_text: Can two Claude Code sessions running simultaneous BrickLayer campaigns on the same machine corrupt each other's questions.md or findings/ files?
rationale: No file locking mechanism exists on questions.md. Two sessions editing the same file simultaneously can cause interleaved writes. The masonry-guard.js hook provides warnings but not atomic locking.

question_text: Does the improve_agent.py optimization loop correctly revert agent instructions when the post-optimization eval score regresses below the pre-optimization baseline?
rationale: The loop explicitly saves a before_snapshot, runs optimization, evaluates after_score, and calls _restore_instructions() if after_score <= before_score. Revert logic is implemented.

question_text: Are Masonry agent prompt optimizations automatically applied to ALL copies of an agent's .md file across the entire repository, including project subdirectories and ~/.claude/agents/?
rationale: writeback_optimized_instructions() explicitly searches: base_dir/.claude/agents/, all first-level subdirectories' .claude/agents/, and ~/.claude/agents/. All copies are updated atomically.

question_text: Does the Masonry eval harness correctly handle Windows path encoding issues when agent prompt files contain Unicode characters outside the cp1252 code page?
rationale: eval_agent.py uses encoding='utf-8' for file reads but subprocess calls may still encounter Windows cp1252 issues depending on terminal configuration. The --system-prompt-file workaround handles long prompts but not encoding edge cases in all code paths.

question_text: If the claude CLI binary is not found on the system PATH, does the gen_training_data.py script fail with a clear error message or silently produce zero training examples?
rationale: Without explicit shutil.which() checks and early exit with a descriptive error, subprocess.run() will raise FileNotFoundError deep in the call stack with a cryptic message. Silent failure or poor error UX is the expected outcome without explicit handling.

question_text: Can the BrickLayer synthesizer agent produce a coherent synthesis document when more than 50% of findings in the findings/ directory have INCONCLUSIVE verdicts?
rationale: The synthesizer is designed to handle mixed verdicts but high INCONCLUSIVE rates degrade synthesis quality — the document will lack definitive conclusions and actionable recommendations, reducing its utility for the campaign operator.

question_text: Does the Masonry agent registry (agent_registry.yml) stay in sync with the actual agent .md files when new agents are added via the masonry-agent-onboard.js hook?
rationale: The onboard hook fires on every Write/Edit to agents/ directories, calls onboard_agent.py which extracts frontmatter and appends a new AgentRegistryEntry with tier:draft. The sync is automatic and event-driven.

question_text: Is there a risk that the improve_agent.py --loops N flag could produce worse results than a single loop due to oscillation between prompt versions?
rationale: Each loop starts from the current (potentially already-optimized) instructions. If loop 1 improves and loop 2 regresses, loop 2 correctly reverts to loop 1's result. However, multiple loops against the same training corpus can lead to overfitting to held-out examples.

question_text: Are BrickLayer project finding files safe from accidental deletion if an agent runs git reset --hard as part of the loop self-recovery procedure?
rationale: git reset --hard HEAD reverts ALL tracked changes. If findings/ files were committed, they are safe. But newly-written findings that haven't been committed yet will be permanently deleted by the reset. The program.md recommends this recovery step without warning about uncommitted findings.

question_text: Does the masonry-stop-guard.js hook correctly identify when a /build autopilot session has pending incomplete tasks and block the Stop event?
rationale: masonry-build-guard.js reads .autopilot/progress.json and checks for tasks with status != DONE. If any exist, it returns a blocking message. The guard fires on every Stop event.
